title: Temporal and spatial patterns of ambient endotoxin concentrations in Fresno, California.
ehr_evidence: The study uses environmental monitoring data (ambient endotoxin and air pollutants) collected from a monitoring site, schools, and residences, rather than EHR or linked health data.
summary: The study characterizes the temporal and spatial variability of ambient endotoxin concentrations in Fresno/Clovis, California, to identify correlates and predictors in a cohort of children with asthma.
key_findings: Endotoxin concentrations were primarily determined by meteorologic factors, particularly air stagnation.
Highest concentrations were found in areas immediately downwind from agricultural/pasture land.
Coarse particulate matter (PMc) was the only pollutant correlated with endotoxin.
Endotoxin was the most spatially variable pollutant measured.
population: Children with asthma in Fresno/Clovis, California (FACES cohort)
limitations: Analyses were restricted to the dry months (May–October)
Short time periods for measurements at some schools and residences
